name: Onboarding — Address Tagged Sanctions/Terrorism/Illicit
description: Reject onboarding if the address itself is tagged as sanctioned, terrorism financing, law enforcement freeze, illicit market, or financial crime. PSN02 Section 6 requires CDD before establishing business relationship.
reference: MAS Notice PSN02 Section 6 (CDD) & FATF Recommendation 10

name: Onboarding — Address Tagged Cybercrime/Obfuscation/Gambling
description: Trigger EDD if the address is tagged as cybercrime, mixer/privacy tool, or unlicensed gambling.

name: Onboarding — Received Funds from Sanctions/Terrorism (Hop 1-3)
description: Reject onboarding if the address has received funds from sanctioned or terrorism-related entities within 3 hops. An address that received dirty money is a risk even if not directly tagged.
reference: MAS Notice PSN02 Section 6 & FATF Recommendation 10

name: Onboarding — Sent Funds to Sanctions/Terrorism (Hop 1-3)
description: Reject onboarding if the address has sent funds to sanctioned or terrorism-related entities within 3 hops. An address that funded sanctioned entities demonstrates intentional involvement and must be rejected.
reference: MAS Notice PSN02 Section 6 & OFAC Prohibition on Providing Indirect Benefits

name: Deposit Inflow — Direct from Sanctions/Terrorism (Hop 1)
description: Freeze deposit if funds come directly from sanctioned, terrorism, frozen, or illicit market entities. Direct interaction indicates intentional or negligent exposure.
reference: MAS Notice PSN02 Section 6 & OFAC SDN Direct Contact

name: Deposit Inflow — Near-Distance Sanctions/Terrorism (Hop 2-3)
description: Freeze deposit if near-distance inflow from sanctions/terrorism entities. Near-distance exposure still represents significant taint.

name: Deposit Inflow — Far-Distance Sanctions/Terrorism (Hop 4-5)
description: Trigger EDD if far-distance inflow from sanctions/terrorism entities. Pollution decay principle: risk reduces with distance but still requires enhanced investigation.
reference: MAS Notice PSN02 & Pollution Decay Principle

name: Deposit Inflow — Cybercrime/Obfuscation/Gambling (Threshold)
description: Trigger EDD if inflow exposure to cybercrime, mixers, gambling, or high-risk entities exceeds 10% AND risk amount > $50 USD.

name: Deposit Inflow — Grey Industry/Spam (Threshold)
description: Review if inflow exposure to grey industry, MEV bots, or spam exceeds 30% AND risk amount > $1,000 USD.

name: Deposit Outflow History — Directly Funded Sanctions/Terrorism (Hop 1)
description: Freeze if the depositing address has directly sent funds to sanctioned/terrorism entities. This indicates the address actively participated in sanctions evasion.
reference: MAS Notice PSN02 & OFAC Prohibition on Providing Indirect Benefits

name: Deposit Outflow History — Indirectly Funded Sanctions/Terrorism (Hop 2-3)
description: Trigger EDD if the depositing address has indirectly funded sanctioned/terrorism entities through intermediaries.
reference: MAS Notice PSN02 & OFAC Indirect Benefits

name: Deposit Self-Tag — Sanctions/Freeze/Illicit
description: Freeze deposit if the depositing address itself is tagged as sanctioned/terrorism/frozen/illicit. Addresses may acquire new risk labels after onboarding due to evolving threat intelligence.

name: Deposit Self-Tag — Cybercrime/Obfuscation
description: Trigger EDD if the depositing address itself is tagged as cybercrime or obfuscation.

name: Whitelist — CEX/TradFi Stop Rule
description: Stop checking further rules for this path if a known compliant CEX or TradFi institution is encountered. Reduces false positives from legitimate exchange flows.
reference: Industry best practice — compliant exchange cut-off

name: Withdrawal — Direct to Sanctions/Terrorism (Hop 1)
description: Reject withdrawal if destination address is directly a sanctioned/terrorism/frozen/illicit entity.
reference: MAS Notice PSN02 Section 8 (STR) & FATF Recommendation 20

name: Withdrawal — Near Sanctions/Terrorism Destination (Hop 2)
description: Freeze withdrawal if 2-hop destination reaches sanctioned/terrorism entities. Prevents using intermediary wallets to circumvent direct sanctions checks.
reference: MAS Notice PSN02 & OFAC Indirect Benefits

name: Withdrawal — Direct to Cybercrime/Mixer (Hop 1)
description: Reject withdrawal to known cybercrime addresses or mixing services.

name: Withdrawal — To Gambling/High-Risk Entity (Hop 1-2)
description: Trigger EDD if withdrawal destination is unlicensed gambling or high-risk entity within 2 hops.

name: Withdrawal Gate — Address Self-Tagged Sanctions/Illicit
description: Freeze withdrawal if the withdrawing address itself is tagged as sanctioned/terrorism/frozen. Prevents sanctioned addresses from moving funds out.

name: Withdrawal Gate — Address Self-Tagged Cybercrime/Obfuscation
description: Trigger EDD if the withdrawing address is tagged as cybercrime or obfuscation before allowing withdrawal.

name: Whitelist — CEX/TradFi Stop Rule
description: Stop checking further rules for this path if destination is a known compliant CEX or TradFi institution.
reference: Industry best practice — compliant exchange cut-off

name: Travel Rule — Transfer > $1,000 USD
description: Flag for Travel Rule compliance when single transfer exceeds $1,000 USD (~S$1,350). Originator and beneficiary information must be recorded per FATF Recommendation 16.
reference: MAS Notice PSN02 Section 14 & FATF Recommendation 16

name: Occasional Transaction KYC Trigger > $3,500 USD
description: Trigger full CDD/KYC for occasional transactions exceeding $3,500 USD (~S$4,700). Per PSN02, DPT providers must conduct CDD for transactions above prescribed threshold.

name: Daily Deposit Volume > $1,100 USD
description: Review alert when daily cumulative deposits exceed $1,100 USD. Potential structuring or smurfing indicator.

name: Daily Deposit Volume > $3,700 USD
description: Trigger EDD when daily cumulative deposits exceed $3,700 USD (~S$5,000). Per PSN02, enhanced CDD required for significant transactions.

name: Daily Withdrawal Volume > $3,700 USD
description: Trigger EDD when daily cumulative withdrawals exceed $3,700 USD (~S$5,000).

name: Daily Withdrawal Volume > $14,800 USD
description: File STR when daily cumulative withdrawals exceed $14,800 USD (~S$20,000). Large value movements require suspicious transaction reporting per PSN02.
